title: Cephalandra grandis
intro:
There is no information about a plant called Cephalandra grandis. However, Cephalandra indica, also known as the ridged gourd or Chinese okra, is a type of plant. 

Cephalandra indica is a type of plant in the Cucurbitaceae family, commonly cultivated for its edible fruit.

The health benefits of Cephalandra indica include its potential to help manage blood sugar levels, aid in digestion, and exhibit anti-inflammatory properties.

The bioactive constituents of Cephalandra indica include cucurbitacins, flavonoids, and ascorbic acid, which contribute to its medicinal properties.

The most common herbal preparations made with Cephalandra indica include teas, decoctions, and infusions, often used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Cephalandra grandis is classified within the family Cucurbitaceae, commonly known as the gourd or cucumber family. This classification places it alongside other cucurbitaceous plants such as cucumbers and squash. The family Cucurbitaceae belongs to the order Cucurbitales, which is a group of flowering plants. Cucurbitales is further classified within the clade of core eudicots, which represents a large and diverse group of flowering plants. This classification reflects the evolutionary relationships between Cephalandra grandis and other plant species.
uses: Cephalandra grandis, also known as the ivory gourd, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's roots are often used to treat digestive issues and diarrhea due to their antidiarrheal properties.
